title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement rgthomas@ucsd.edu. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to zzrenvcheck
text: Thank interest contributing. document describes expected workflow proposing changes, reporting issues, submitting pull requests. zzrenvcheck ships two parallel implementations: R package (R/, exposing check_packages() friends). standalone POSIX shell script (modules/validation.sh, installable via install.sh). use validation logic. change validation behaviour, please update pull request drift.

title: Contributing to zzrenvcheck
text: opening issue, please search existing issues https://github.com/rgt47/zzrenvcheck/issues confirm problem already reported. opening new issue, include: minimal reproducible example (reprex::reprex() R, copy project structure command shell script). output sessionInfo() (R) bash --version OS version (shell). version zzrenvcheck use. expected behaviour observed behaviour.

title: Contributing to zzrenvcheck
text: Fork repository create topic branch main. Install development dependencies: Make changes. R changes, update function roxygen2 block. shell changes, update modules/validation.sh ensure POSIX compatibility (bashisms). Add update tests tests/testthat/. R test suite covers parsing, extraction, CRAN/Bioconductor/GitHub validation. Run devtools::document() regenerate man/ NAMESPACE. Run full check locally: shell-script changes, run shellcheck modules/validation.sh verify supported targets. Update NEWS.md one-line bullet unreleased section. Open pull request main. Reference related issues.

title: Contributing to zzrenvcheck
text: Use native R pipe (|>); avoid %>% new code. Use <- assignment, never =. Use snake_case functions variables. Prefer implicit returns; reserve return() early exits. Document exported functions roxygen2. must @title, @description, @param, @return, @examples. Two-space indentation. Single quotes character literals.

title: Contributing to zzrenvcheck
text: POSIX-compatible (#!/usr/bin/env bash justified). shellcheck must pass. Two-space indentation, snake_case variables. Quote variable expansions.

title: Quickstart Guide: zzrenvcheck
text: Reproducibility requires package resolve compatible versions everywhere named. check_packages() compares version recorded DESCRIPTION constraints, renv.lock, version-pinned installs found code, reports mismatch. comparison constraint-aware: exact version must satisfy DESCRIPTION constraint, two exact pins must agree. Consider project whose DESCRIPTION requires dplyr (>= 2.0.0), whose renv.lock records dplyr 1.1.0, whose code pins third version pak::pak('dplyr@1.2.0'). Running check_packages() reports: Pins read pak renv @-syntax (including vectorised multi-argument pak() calls), remotes/devtools install_version(), DESCRIPTION Remotes: field. reproducibility files Dockerfile, install.sh, Makefile, .Rprofile scanned addition source directories. check report-; never rewrites versions. Disable check_packages(versions = FALSE). Conflicts returned version_conflicts element result. Two forms deliberately compared: pak requirement keyword refs (pkg@>=1.6.0, pkg@last, pkg@current), pin exact version; pins split across multiple lines, scanning line-based. standalone shell script performs version-consistency check every run. reports identical conflicts exits non-zero found, extra flag required:
code: ── Version Conflicts ── x Found 1 package with inconsistent versions across DESCRIPTION,   renv.lock, and code ! dplyr: code pin 1.2.0 != renv.lock 1.1.0; renv.lock 1.1.0 violates   DESCRIPTION (>= 2.0.0) i Reproducibility requires matching versions across all sources. # Report-only validation, including version conflicts zzrenvcheck --no-fix  # ── Version Conflicts ── # dplyr: code pin 1.2.0 != renv.lock 1.1.0; renv.lock 1.1.0 violates #   DESCRIPTION (>= 2.0.0)

Features: R installation required Uses grep, sed, awk, jq, curl Perfect CI/CD pipelines

title: renv vs zzrenvcheck: Complementary Tools for Reproducible Dependencies
text: reproducible R project keeps three descriptions dependencies agreement: code: packages scripts, functions, tests, reports actually load; DESCRIPTION file: packages project formally declares Imports Suggests; renv.lock file: exact package versions pinned reproduction. renv zzrenvcheck serve reproducibility, govern different relationships among three. complementary, alternatives. renv manages environment. reconciles installed library lockfile, can infer packages code uses. question : installed lockfile records, can environment recreated elsewhere? zzrenvcheck audits declarations. compares code, DESCRIPTION, renv.lock, reports disagree. question : every package code uses declared DESCRIPTION pinned renv.lock, anything declared code longer uses? renv notion DESCRIPTION; zzrenvcheck neither installs packages manages library. Neither tool subsumes .

title: renv vs zzrenvcheck: Complementary Tools for Reproducible Dependencies
text: renv operates library lockfile: renv::status() closest renv comes validation: reports installed library, lockfile, packages used code fall sync. read DESCRIPTION, judge whether declared dependency still used.
code: renv::init()        # create a project-private library and renv.lock renv::snapshot()    # record installed versions into renv.lock renv::restore()     # install the versions renv.lock records renv::status()      # report drift among library, lockfile, and used code renv::dependencies() # list packages detected in the code

title: renv vs zzrenvcheck: Complementary Tools for Reproducible Dependencies
text: zzrenvcheck performs three-way consistency check, dependency triad: scans code packages actually used (R/, strict = TRUE also tests/ vignettes/), compares set DESCRIPTION renv.lock. reports three kinds mismatch: used code DESCRIPTION – undeclared dependency; DESCRIPTION renv.lock – reproducibility break, since collaborator restoring lockfile receive package; DESCRIPTION used code – stale declaration. auto_fix = TRUE adds missing entries (validating, default, installable CRAN, Bioconductor, GitHub first); cleanup = TRUE also removes unused declarations.
code: zzrenvcheck::check_packages()                    # report only zzrenvcheck::check_packages(auto_fix = TRUE)     # add missing declarations zzrenvcheck::check_packages(cleanup = TRUE)      # also remove unused zzrenvcheck::check_packages(strict = FALSE)      # skip tests/ and vignettes/

what: A case each tool catches and the other misses
title: renv vs zzrenvcheck: Complementary Tools for Reproducible Dependencies
text: tools complementary sees relationship . renv satisfied, zzrenvcheck . function gains library(glue) call. renv::snapshot() detects glue, installs , pins renv.lock; renv::status() clean. glue never added DESCRIPTION. project also R package, R CMD check warn, collaborator reading DESCRIPTION know glue required. zzrenvcheck::check_packages() reports glue used code declared DESCRIPTION. zzrenvcheck one looking. last use stringr deleted code, Imports entry remains. renv tracks library lockfile, declarations, flag declared--unused dependency. zzrenvcheck reports stringr declared unused, cleanup = TRUE removes . declared package never reaches lockfile. dependency added DESCRIPTION yet called anywhere, renv, locks code uses, reason record . zzrenvcheck flags present DESCRIPTION missing renv.lock – latent reproducibility gap.

title: renv vs zzrenvcheck: Complementary Tools for Reproducible Dependencies
text: two tools form single workflow rather choice: renv keeps environment reproducible; zzrenvcheck keeps project’s dependency declarations honest. zzcollab compendium validation runs inside project’s Docker container, example make check-renv, depend R installed host.
code: # 1. develop, installing packages inside the renv project # 2. pin exact versions renv::snapshot() # 3. bring DESCRIPTION and renv.lock into agreement with the code zzrenvcheck::check_packages(auto_fix = TRUE) # 4. confirm the library still matches the lockfile renv::status()

title: renv vs zzrenvcheck: Complementary Tools for Reproducible Dependencies
text: Use renv build reproduce environment, zzrenvcheck keep code, DESCRIPTION, renv.lock telling story.

title: zzrenvcheck
text: Validate R Package Dependencies Reproducibility

title: zzrenvcheck
text: zzrenvcheck ensures R packages used project code properly declared DESCRIPTION locked renv.lock, maintaining reproducible environments collaborative research. Problem: R projects often undocumented dependencies, leading : ‘Package found’ errors collaborators Broken reproducibility Manual dependency management Solution: zzrenvcheck automatically: Scans code package usage (library(), require(), pkg::function()) Validates DESCRIPTION renv.lock consistency Auto-fixes missing packages via CRAN/Bioconductor/GitHub validation Syncs dependencies code (removes unused, adds missing) Works cross-platform (Windows/macOS/Linux)

title: zzrenvcheck
text: local clone:
code: # From GitHub # install.packages('pak') pak::pak('rgt47/zzrenvcheck') R CMD INSTALL .

title: zzrenvcheck
text: Suitable CI/CD, Docker environments, systems without R: standalone version: Works without R installation Uses shell tools (grep, sed, awk, jq, curl) Suitable CI/CD pipelines Can validate auto-fix via CRAN API Runs version-synchronization check R package (DESCRIPTION vs renv.lock vs code pins) exits non-zero conflict, zzrenvcheck ---fix works CI gate
code: # Clone the repository git clone https://github.com/rgt47/zzrenvcheck.git cd zzrenvcheck  # Install to ~/bin (default) ./install.sh  # Or install to custom location ./install.sh --prefix ~/.local  # Or install with custom name ./install.sh --name check-packages  # Now use it anywhere (if ~/bin is in your PATH) zzrenvcheck --fix --strict --verbose

title: zzrenvcheck
code: library(zzrenvcheck)  # Check your project check_packages()  # Auto-fix missing packages fix_packages()  # View detailed report report <- report_packages() print(report)

title: zzrenvcheck
text: Finds packages multiple sources: - library(dplyr) - Direct library calls - require(ggplot2) - Conditional loading - tidyr::pivot_longer() - Namespace calls - #' @importFrom dplyr filter - Roxygen imports

title: zzrenvcheck
text: Beyond checking package declared, check_packages() checks version consistent across DESCRIPTION constraints, renv.lock, version-pinned installs code. Comparison constraint-aware: exact version must satisfy DESCRIPTION constraint, two exact pins must agree. Version pins read pak renv @-syntax (pak::pak('dplyr@1.1.0'), including vectorised multi-argument calls), remotes/devtools install_version(), DESCRIPTION Remotes: field. addition usual source directories, reproducibility files Dockerfile, install.sh, Makefile, .Rprofile scanned, pinned installs commonly drift renv.lock. check report-(never rewrites versions) controlled versions argument (default TRUE). Conflicts returned version_conflicts element set result status fail. Two forms deliberately compared: pak requirement keyword refs (pkg@>=1.6.0, pkg@last, pkg@current), exact pins; pins split across multiple lines, scanning line-based.
code: check_packages()  # ── Version Conflicts ── # x Found 1 package with inconsistent versions across DESCRIPTION, #   renv.lock, and code # ! dplyr: code pin 1.2.0 != renv.lock 1.1.0; renv.lock 1.1.0 violates #   DESCRIPTION (>= 2.0.0) # i Reproducibility requires matching versions across all sources.

text: Developed zzcollab team.
code: @software{zzrenvcheck,   title = {zzrenvcheck: Validate R Package Dependencies for Reproducibility},   author = {Thomas, Ronald G.},   url = {https://github.com/rgt47/zzrenvcheck} }

title: Base R Packages — BASE_PACKAGES
text: Base R packages need explicit declaration DESCRIPTION. packages always available R installations.

title: Code Version-Pin Install Forms — CODE_PIN_PATTERNS
text: Patterns used extract_code_package_versions() recognise version-pinned package installs. Two grammars supported: @-syntax: pak::pak('dplyr@1.1.0'), pak('dplyr@1.1.0'), pak::pkg_install('dplyr@1.1.0'), renv::install('dplyr@1.1.0'). version= syntax: remotes::install_version('dplyr', version = '1.1.0'), devtools::install_version('dplyr', version = '1.1.0'). @-syntax, pin_call_at detects line contains install call at_token extracts every 'pkg@version' token line, vectorised (pak(c('@1', 'b@2'))) multi-argument calls captured. version token must begin digit, excludes GitHub refs (owner/repo@branch).

title: File Extensions to Search — FILE_EXTENSIONS
text: R source file extensions scan package references.

title: Placeholder Package Names — PLACEHOLDER_PACKAGES
text: Invalid placeholder package names exclude validation. commonly used documentation examples treated real package references.

what: Reproducibility Files Scanned for Version Pins — REPRO_FILES
title: Reproducibility Files Scanned for Version Pins — REPRO_FILES
text: Non-R project files commonly carry version-pinned install commands can therefore drift renv.lock. scanned extract_code_package_versions() (version-synchronisation check); deliberately excluded plain package-name scan, build tooling shell commands generate false positives.

title: Reproducibility Files Scanned for Version Pins — REPRO_FILES

what: Directories to Skip During Scanning — SKIP_DIRS
title: Directories to Skip During Scanning — SKIP_DIRS
text: Directory patterns exclude package scanning.

title: Directories to Skip During Scanning — SKIP_DIRS

what: Files to Skip During Scanning — SKIP_FILES
title: Files to Skip During Scanning — SKIP_FILES
text: Documentation example files scanned package dependencies (often contain example code).

title: Files to Skip During Scanning — SKIP_FILES
Standard Directories to Scan — STANDARD_DIRS
text: Default directories scanned standard (non-strict) mode.

title: Strict Mode Directories — STRICT_DIRS
text: directories scanned strict mode (includes tests vignettes).

text: packages Character vector. Package names check. check_cran Logical. Check CRAN. Default: TRUE. check_bioc Logical. Check Bioconductor. Default: TRUE. check_github Logical. Check GitHub. Default: TRUE. progress Logical. Show progress. Default: TRUE.

title: Check Multiple Packages for Installability — check_installable
text: data frame columns: package, installable, source

title: Check Multiple Packages for Installability — check_installable
Check Package Dependencies — check_packages
text: Validates R packages used code properly declared DESCRIPTION locked renv.lock reproducibility.

title: Check Package Dependencies — check_packages
code: check_packages(   strict = TRUE,   auto_fix = FALSE,   cleanup = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   validate_sources = auto_fix,   transitive = FALSE,   versions = TRUE,   error_on_fail = FALSE,   path = "." )

title: Check Package Dependencies — check_packages
text: strict Logical. TRUE, scans tests/ vignettes/ directories. Default: TRUE. auto_fix Logical. TRUE, automatically adds missing packages. Default: FALSE. cleanup Logical. TRUE, syncs code (adds missing, removes unused). Equivalent calling sync_packages(). Default: FALSE. verbose Logical. TRUE, lists issues found. Default: TRUE. validate_sources Logical. TRUE, checks packages installable CRAN/Bioconductor/GitHub adding. Default: TRUE auto_fix. transitive Logical. TRUE, also resolve add transitive dependencies fixing renv.lock. Default: FALSE. versions Logical. TRUE, check package versions consistent across DESCRIPTION constraints, renv.lock, code install pins. Default: TRUE. error_on_fail Logical. TRUE, raise error (rather returning) validation fails, non-interactive Rscript run exits non-zero status. signalled condition class zzrenvcheck_validation_failure carries result list result field. Default: FALSE. path Character. Path project root. Default: current directory.

title: Check Package Dependencies — check_packages
text: Invisibly returns list validation results

title: Check Package Dependencies — check_packages
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic validation check_packages()  # Auto-fix missing packages check_packages(auto_fix = TRUE)  # Cleanup mode: sync to code (remove unused, add missing) check_packages(cleanup = TRUE)  # Non-strict mode (skip tests and vignettes) check_packages(strict = FALSE) } # }

what: Clean Unused Packages from DESCRIPTION — clean_description
title: Clean Unused Packages from DESCRIPTION — clean_description
text: Removes packages DESCRIPTION Imports used code. helps keep DESCRIPTION aligned actual dependencies.

title: Clean Unused Packages from DESCRIPTION — clean_description

title: Detect Cross-Document Version Conflicts — detect_version_conflicts
text: Compares versions recorded package across DESCRIPTION constraints, renv.lock exact versions, exact pins drawn code DESCRIPTION Remotes: field, applying constraint-aware rules. package reported following holds: two pins package disagree (across within sources); pin differs renv.lock version; renv.lock version violates DESCRIPTION constraint; pin violates DESCRIPTION constraint. Packages version data compare, whose constraint "*", never appear.

title: Detect Cross-Document Version Conflicts — detect_version_conflicts
text: desc_deps Data frame parse_description_all_deps() columns package, type, version. lock_versions Data frame parse_renv_lock_versions() columns package, version. code_versions Data frame exact pins columns package, version, optionally source (label "code" "DESCRIPTION Remotes"). source absent every pin labelled "code".

title: Detect Cross-Document Version Conflicts — detect_version_conflicts

title: zzrenvcheck: Validate R Package Dependencies for Reproducibility — zzrenvcheck-package
text: Maintainer: Ronald (Ryy) G Thomas rgthomas@ucsd.edu (ORCID) Authors: Ronald (Ryy) G Thomas rgthomas@ucsd.edu (ORCID)

title: zzrenvcheck v0.4.0
text: check_packages() now validates version consistency across DESCRIPTION constraints, renv.lock, version-pinned installs code. Reproducibility requires package resolve compatible versions everywhere; mismatch (example DESCRIPTION requires >= 2.0.0 renv.lock records 1.1.0) reported new ‘Version Conflicts’ section. Comparison constraint-aware: exact version must satisfy DESCRIPTION constraint, two exact pins must agree. check report-controlled new versions argument (default TRUE). Results returned version_conflicts element. Added extract_code_package_versions(), extracts version-pinned installs code. Recognised forms pak renv @-syntax (pak::pak('dplyr@1.1.0'), including vectorised multi-argument calls) remotes/devtools install_version() function (named, positional, package =/version = argument shapes). addition usual scanned directories, reproducibility files (Dockerfile, install.sh, Makefile, .Rprofile) scanned pins, since commonly drift renv.lock. Version-like pins DESCRIPTION Remotes: field (example owner/repo@v1.1.0) parsed reconciled renv.lock. Added error_on_fail argument check_packages(). TRUE, failing validation raises zzrenvcheck_validation_failure condition non-interactive Rscript run exits non-zero, matching shell script’s behaviour. Default FALSE preserves returned-result behaviour. shell validator (modules/validation.sh) mirrors check_version_conflicts() exits non-zero conflict. Corrected -broad path filter: files skipped ancestor directory name merely contained skip token (example renv); filter now matches path segment.

title: zzrenvcheck v0.4.0
text: pak requirement keyword refs (pkg@>=1.6.0, pkg@last, pkg@current) exact pins compared; exact pkg@version pins checked. Scanning line-based, version pin split across multiple lines (example pak::pak(c(...)) call one package per line) detected.

title: zzrenvcheck v0.3.2
text: Added vignette, ‘renv vs zzrenvcheck’, explaining two tools differ complement (renv manages environment; zzrenvcheck audits code/DESCRIPTION/renv.lock declarations). renv now listed Suggests vignette. Fixed check_packages() sync_packages() adding workspace’s package DESCRIPTION Imports report calls library(<own_pkg>). package can longer made depend (self-cycle R CMD check rejects). Fixed crash check_installable() reporting progress (‘find current progress bar’); progress-bar id now passed explicitly. Network-dependent tests now also require configured CRAN mirror, R CMD check longer errors run without one. Documented transitive argument added missing utils stats imports. R CMD check passes errors, warnings, notes.

title: zzrenvcheck v0.3.0
text: Initial public release.
